scorecardMd:
# Launch Rehearsal — Scorecard

| Field | Value |
|-------|--------|
| **Run ID** | `cal-20260529-213154` |
| **Date** | 2026-05-29 |
| **Target** | https://cal.com |
| **Product** | Cal.com (practice target) |
| **Outcome** | complete |
| **Duration** | 65s |

---

## Executive summary

| | |
|---|---|
| **Readiness** | **Green** |
| **Top blocker** | Duplicate primary CTAs |
| **Top delight** | Clear navigation structure |
| **Issues** | 5 |
| **Delights** | 6 |


---

## Persona × journey matrix

| Journey | P1 First-time | P2 Daily | P3 Admin |
|---------|--------|--------|--------|
| **j1-land** Land and grasp value | **Pass** | **Pass** | **Pass** |
| **j2-signup-path** Start signup path | **Pass** | **Pass** | **Pass** |
| **j3-product-depth** Explore product depth | **Partial** | **Pass** | **Partial** |
| **j4-pricing** Pricing evaluation | **Pass** | **Partial** | **Pass** |
| **j5-return-nav** Return visitor navigation | **Pass** | **Pass** | **Pass** |

---

## Multi-agent collaboration

| Agent | Role | Summary |
|-------|------|---------|
| `journey-runner` | E2E journey execution | Executed 8 steps across 5 journeys (0 failures) |
| `persona-p1-evaluator` | Evaluator: First-time evaluator (prospect / founder) | Analysis from prospect / founder perspective |
| `persona-p2-operator` | Evaluator: Daily operator (power user) | Analysis from power user perspective |
| `persona-p3-admin` | Evaluator: Admin / buyer (team admin) | Analysis from team admin perspective |
| `llm-p1-evaluator` | LLM evaluator: First-time evaluator | The landing page clearly communicates Cal.com's value proposition and offers mul |
| `llm-p2-operator` | LLM evaluator: Daily operator | The landing page and pricing page load quickly and are clearly structured, but t |
| `llm-p3-admin` | LLM evaluator: Admin / buyer | Admin can land on the homepage and navigate to pricing and enterprise pages, but |
| `synthesizer` | Cross-agent synthesis | Synthesized 5 issues, 6 delights from 7 agent reports |

---

## Dimension rollup (automated subset)

| Dimension | Score 1–5 | Signal |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| Functionality | 5 | 100% steps pass; 0 failures |
| UI/UX | 5 | 0 unlabeled buttons; ~1766ms avg step |
| Information clarity | 5 | 0 sparse-content pages |

---

## Issues (evidence-bound)

### P2

| ID | Finding | Personas | Evidence |
|----|---------|----------|----------|
| **I2** | Enterprise link leads to generic landing page instead of dedicated enterprise content — When clicking the 'Enterprise' link in the navigation, the user is taken to the same generic landing page (https://cal.com/enterprise) that shows the same content as the homepage, rather than a page with enterprise-specific features, case studies, or pricing. This fails to provide the product depth expected by a first-time evaluator trying to understand the product's value for larger organizations. | p1-evaluator | `j3-product-depth-p1-evaluator-s2` |
| **I3** | Pricing page lacks a direct sign-up CTA for the free plan — On the pricing page, the 'Individuals Free' plan is described but has no 'Sign up' or 'Get started' button next to it. Users must navigate back to the home page or use the top-right 'Get started' button, adding friction for a power user who wants to quickly sign up for the free tier. | p2-operator | `j4-pricing-p1-evaluator-s1` |
| **I4** | Enterprise page shows generic homepage content instead of enterprise-specific details — When navigating to /enterprise (step j3-product-depth-p1-evaluator-s2), the page title and excerpt are identical to the homepage, lacking enterprise features, case studies, or security/compliance information needed for admin evaluation. | p3-admin | `j3-product-depth-p1-evaluator-s2` |

### P3

| ID | Finding | Personas | Evidence |
|----|---------|----------|----------|
| **I1** | Duplicate primary CTAs (hypothesis) — Detected 5 similar signup CTAs — may confuse return visitors. | p2-operator | `j1-land-p1-evaluator-s1` |
| **I5** | Pricing page does not prominently display enterprise plan details — The pricing page (j4-pricing-p1-evaluator-s1) excerpt mentions 'Talk to sales' but does not show enterprise plan pricing or feature breakdown, requiring admin to take extra steps to compare. | p3-admin | `j4-pricing-p1-evaluator-s1` |

---

## Delights & strengths (required)

| ID | Delight | Personas | Evidence |
|----|---------|----------|----------|
| **D1** | Clear navigation structure — Page has 92 links and 29 headings. | p1-evaluator, p2-operator | `j1-land-p1-evaluator-s1` |
| **D2** | Informative landing content — Page includes headings and substantive body copy for first-time evaluators. | p1-evaluator | `j1-land-p1-evaluator-s1` |
| **D3** | Fast interactions for repeat tasks — 7 steps completed under 3s. | p2-operator | `j2-signup-path-p1-evaluator-s1` |
| **D4** | Clear value proposition and multiple sign-up options on landing page — The landing page immediately communicates the product's value ('The better way to schedule your meetings') and offers both Google and email sign-up options with a 'No credit card required' callout, making it easy for a first-time evaluator to start. | p1-evaluator | `j1-land-p1-evaluator-s1` |
| **D5** | Clear and fast-loading pricing page with feature breakdown — The pricing page loads quickly (1664 ms) and presents a clear 'See feature breakdown' link, helping power users compare plans efficiently. | p2-operator | `j4-pricing-p1-evaluator-s1` |
| **D6** | Clear top navigation with direct links to Enterprise and Pricing — The homepage (j1-land-p1-evaluator-s1) includes visible navigation items for 'Enterprise' and 'Pricing', making it easy for an admin to start comparing plans. | p3-admin | `j1-land-p1-evaluator-s1` |

---

## Run log

| Step | Journey | Action | Outcome | URL |
|------|---------|--------|---------|-----|
| `j1-land-p1-evaluator-s1` | j1-land | navigate | pass | https://cal.com/ |
| `j2-signup-path-p1-evaluator-s1` | j2-signup-path | navigate | pass | https://cal.com/ |
| `j2-signup-path-p1-evaluator-s2` | j2-signup-path | click | pass | https://cal.com/ |
| `j3-product-depth-p1-evaluator-s1` | j3-product-depth | navigate | pass | https://cal.com/ |
| `j3-product-depth-p1-evaluator-s2` | j3-product-depth | click | pass | https://cal.com/enterprise |
| `j4-pricing-p1-evaluator-s1` | j4-pricing | navigate | pass | https://cal.com/pricing |
| `j5-return-nav-p1-evaluator-s1` | j5-return-nav | navigate | pass | https://cal.com/pricing |
| `j5-return-nav-p1-evaluator-s2` | j5-return-nav | navigate | pass | https://cal.com/ |

---

narrative.executiveSummary: Argyle Faculty Dashboard at https://faculty-dashboard-eight.vercel.app/database rehearsed as **Red** readiness. 5/5 canonical steps passed (100%). 3 issue(s) and 2 delight(s) recorded. Primary concern: Duplicate primary CTAs. Bright spot: Clear navigation structure.
narrative.forFounders:
**Launch readiness: Red** — suitable for internal review before external calls.
Broken journeys: Use Process Updates to manage faculty data workflows, Use Resume Parser to extract data from a candidate's resume, Add a new faculty member manually, Admin panel system management.
narrative.forEngineering:

Notable P2: Console errors on page; Console noise spike across run.
suggestedQuestions: What is the single blocker we should fix before the next partner call?
Why did Use Process Updates to manage faculty data workflows fail?

layer1Verdict.headline: Do not ship this build.
layer1Verdict.reason: 0 blocker(s) will break user journeys in production.

narrative.layer2Story: TryLapse sent 10 AI personas through 24 product journeys and scored **5/5** steps as passing. 24 journeys — Use Process Updates to manage faculty data workflows, Use Resume Parser to extract data from a candidate's resume — failed completely, meaning users would be blocked before completing their goal. 2 genuine product strength(s) found: Clear navigation structure.
